Crataegus monogyna, commonly known as Hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree with a long history of traditional medicinal use. The health benefits of Hawthorn include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiotonic properties. The bioactive constituents of Hawthorn include flavonoids (such as quercetin, vitexin, and rutin), oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), and triterpenoids (such as ursolic acid and oleanolic acid). The most common herbal preparations made with Hawthorn include teas, tinctures, capsules, and tablets, which are often used to support heart health and alleviate symptoms of cardiovascular disease.
